search_queries_used: site:academic.oup.com ESE adrenal incidentaloma 2023 recommendations imaging hormone excess follow-up
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
The revised 2023 guideline was selected instead.
Result interpretation was left to clinicians.
population_applicability: Partial: age, pregnancy, frailty, lesion, hormonal, and malignancy-risk qualifiers are not fully represented in the legacy workflow.
setting_applicability: Partial: exact for incidentaloma assessment and qualified follow-up, not diagnosis, surgery, or treatment.
UAE_applicability: No exact UAE adrenal incidentaloma guideline was identified. ESE recommendations require UAE endocrine, radiology, and referral-pathway review.
recency_verification: The official 2023 EJE guideline was reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: The revised 2023 ESE guideline is current; the 2016 predecessor was not used.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped incidentaloma identity, imaging-report, and qualified follow-up-imaging documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports imaging-report review, CT or MRI context, lesion characteristics, qualified follow-up imaging, and multidisciplinary discussion.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinical hormone-excess, blood-pressure, hormonal-workup, and electrolyte documentation with qualifiers retained.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinical signs of hormone excess, blood pressure and hypertension, diabetes context, recommendation-qualified hormonal workup, and electrolytes or hypokalaemia context.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-arranged referral and follow-up without automatic urgency or fixed timing.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports qualified clinician-arranged multidisciplinary review, referral, hormonal reassessment, and imaging follow-up without prescribing timing universally.

unresolved_source_gaps: The workflow does not encode all lesion, imaging, biochemical, age, pregnancy, frailty, or malignancy-risk qualifiers.

No-acute-symptom wording, general appearance, weight, weight change, bruising or striae, weakness or fatigue, family history, medicine history, previous records, generic clinician plan, fixed follow-up intervals, PRN wording, and the combined history draft remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, hormone-result interpretation, imaging classification, surgery, treatment, medicine selection, urgency, and exact follow-up timing remain clinician responsibilities.
